_comment: NGB verify: identify pass on models that passed the canonical 2026-05-05 NGB explain pass; verifies correctness (numeric_status / numeric_max_diff fields, populated by identify pass per sweep/worker.py:3793-3822) under nested_graph_breaks=true + fullgraph=false on the current stack. Anchored on sweep_results/experiments/nested-gb-2026-05-05-2026-05-05/explain_results.json (canonical NGB experiment per the 2026-05-06 report at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qDcd-q4qZZMVIMfMCJt2aFfpcE0-D0P-V0lhP59bIgk/edit). Both 2026-05-06 NGB runs are ABANDONED — do not anchor on them.
name: ngb-verify-2026-05-07
description: NGB verify on cohort of models that passed the canonical 2026-05-05 NGB explain pass.

models._comment: Reads the 2026-05-05 explain pass results (status==ok subset) via the corpus_filter `from` extension (landed 2026-05-07). source_sha256 pins the source file's content; validation refuses launch on drift.

configs._comment: Single config — NGB verify is one experiment, not an A/B. fullgraph=false is REQUIRED: NGB allows breaks; fullgraph=true would refuse to compile when breaks happen.
configs.name: ngb

settings._comment_pass: 1 = identify (which now ALSO populates numeric_status / numeric_max_diff for correctness checking — verified in worker.py:3793-3822 + abandoned NGB run's identify_results.json). No need for pass 2 (explain) — that adds break_reasons logging, separate concern.
settings._comment_pinning: python_bin + modellib_pins are the canonical stack for this experiment. tools/derive_sweep_commands.py REQUIRES these fields and bakes them into the emitted bash so the launch can't drift to a different stack.
